title: Introduction To The Songs Of Innocence by william blake
description:
Few poems in English literature carry the weight of an entire philosophical vision in just a few lines, but William Blake's "Introduction" to Songs of Innocence does exactly that — a piper on a cloud, a laughing child, and suddenly the whole world of innocent joy is summoned into existence. This deceptively simple lyric, essential for BA English Honours students at DU, SOL, and IGNOU, quietly introduces Blake's central tension between innocence and experience through the act of creation itself. If you've ever wondered why Blake chose a child to command the poet — not a king, not a god — this video will change how you read every poem that follows.

Introduction to the songs of innocence by William blake for 1st sem

‘Introduction to the Songs of Innocence’ is the first poem in William Blake’s collection of poetry the ‘Songs of Innocence’ written in 1789. The poems present in this collection expresses a naive, childlike view of salvation, as most of the poems are addressed to children. In his simple perspective of life, the world is beautiful and Jesus died for our sins. This poem titled ‘Introduction’ sets the tone for the entire sequence. The poet is projected as a visionary who is divinely inspired through angels. ‘Introduction’ also reflects the process of poetic composition. It defines the composition of poetry as a process of making what was formless to artistic creation.
